title: Sierra College - NCC 30th Anniverary Legacy Art Show
description: Celebrate 30 years of Sierra College - NCC with the presentation of the collection of artwork from our faculty and alumni. , In the Granucci Gallery at the Center for the Arts, the show runs from April 29 - June 5., Everyone welcome to the opening reception on April 30 from 5-7pm.

title: "VISIONS OF LIFE"
description: The Nevada Theatre is thrilled to present,“Visions of Life,” a special film event featuring all-new environmental films by local filmmaker Don Baldwin. The evening is planned as a fundraiser for our area’s Interfaith Food Ministry. Special program features include the musical gift of local guitarist Jim Greeninger and stunning night sky and Aurora Borealis p

title: North Mississippi All-Stars
description: North Mississippi Allstars bring their Grammy-nominated blend of blues and rock to The Center for the Arts, May 6., Experience raw Mississippi grooves and high-energy live performance., More information at thecenterforthearts.org.

title: Floralia
description: Beautiful floral-themed artwork by a bevy of talented, local artists adorns the walls of the Seven Stars Gallery for the month of May. Artist reception takes place on Friday, May 8th from 6-8 pm and includes a “fancy hat” contest.

title: Rashomon
description: Community Asian Theatre of the Sierra presents Rashomon, a play that explores the notions of truth and justice – a “who done it?” in medieval Japan -- challenging perceptions of eye witnesses. title: Brandon Dannals live at Lucchesi's Tasting Room
description: 117 Mill Street, Grass Valley, CA 95945 Saturday, May 9, 2026, 5 – 8pm Brandon Dannal the Human Jute Box, will be joining us in the Vintage store this Saturday to sing in Spring. He will perform from 5 until 8. Price : free Phone : 530-263-8304 Email : lindac@lucchesivineyards.com Type of event : Music Venue : Vintage on Mill Event location : Lucchesi Vineya

title: Gentlemen Prefer Blondes (1953)
description: The career of American director Howard Hawks spanned over four decades, from the silent era of the 1920’s to his last completed feature film in 1970. The Onyx Downtown spotlights the legendary career of this Hollywood auteur with three of his indelible classics in three distinctive genres: screwball comedy, film noir, and the movie musical. title: African Drum and Dance of Nevada County
description: Come join our vibrant African Drum and Dance classes! Open to all ages and abilities, we explore different styles from across the African Diasporic disciplines. Including Malian, Congolese, Guinean, Senegalese, Afro-Cuban and Afro-Brazilian. Masters of their craft travel from all over to share their culture and heritage with our beautiful community, along wi

title: Eric Johnson- Texaphonic Tour 2026
description: Eric Johnson is widely recognized as one of the most extraordinary instrumentalists in rock over the past four decades. A Texas guitar legend long before his first album, he earned early comparisons to Jeff Beck and Jimi Hendrix, and his fusion band Electromagnets was hailed as “the Mahavishnu Orchestra of Texas.”, His 1986 debut Tones earned a Grammy nomina

title: Carved Wood Relief and Sign Carving
description: Carved Wood Relief and Sign Carving with Master Carver Ron Ramsey, Experience the fun and delight of creating a dimensional relief woodcarving.
